query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 4b62df58).

## Issue to Resolve

False positive on indent with try/catch and typealias
## Expected Behavior
The following code is valid:

```
fun test() = try {
  1
}
catch(_: Throwable) {
  2
}
```

and

```
typealias Foo =
  HashMap<Bar, Baz?>
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint formats the code like this:

```
fun test() = try {
  1
}
  catch(_: Throwable) {
    2
  }
```

and

```
typealias Foo =
HashMap<Bar, Baz?>
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run `ktlint --format`

.editorconfig:

```
[*.{kt,kts}]
ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false
ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
indent_size = 2
indent_style = space
insert_final_newline = true
ktlint_experimental = enabled
ktlint_experimental_comment-wrapping = disabled
ktlint_experimental_function-naming = disabled
ktlint_experimental_function-signature = disabled
ktlint_experimental_property-naming = disabled
ktlint_standard_annotation = disabled
ktlint_standard_filename = disabled
ktlint_standard_keyword-spacing = disabled
ktlint_standard_max-line-length = disabled
ktlint_standard_no-semi = disabled
ktlint_standard_package-name = disabled
ktlint_standard_spacing-between-declarations-with-annotations = disabled
ktlint_standard_string-template = disabled
max_line_length = 120
```

The formatting is correct when ktlint 0.47.1 is used

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.2
* Operating System and version: Ubuntu 22.04

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

117→public class IndentationRule :
118→    StandardRule(
119→        id = "indent",
120→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
121→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
122→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
123→                ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
124→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
125→            ),
126→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
127→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
128→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
129→            ),
130→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
131→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
132→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
133→            ),
134→        ),
135→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
136→            CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
137→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
138→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
139→        ),
140→    ) {
141→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
142→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
143→
144→    private var line = 1
145→
146→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
147→
148→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
149→
150→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
151→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
152→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
153→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
154→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
155→        )
156→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
157→            stopTraversalOfAST()
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
162→        node: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→    ) {
166→        if (node.isRoot()) {
167→            // File should not start with a whitespace
168→            node
169→                .nextLeaf()
170→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
171→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
172→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
173→                    if (autoCorrect) {
174→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
175→                    }
176→                }
177→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
178→        }
179→
180→        when {
181→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
182→                line++
183→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
184→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
185→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
186→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
187→                    )
188→                }
189→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
190→            }
191→
192→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
193→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
194→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
195→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
196→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
197→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
198→                startIndentContext(
199→                    fromAstNode = node,
200→                    lastChildIndent = "",
201→                )
202→
203→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
204→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
205→
206→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
207→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
208→                startIndentContext(node)
209→
210→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
211→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
212→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
213→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ->
214→                startIndentContext(node)
215→
216→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
217→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
218→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
219→                startIndentContext(
220→                    fromAstNode = node,
221→                    childIndent = "",
222→                )
223→
224→            node.elementType == IF ->
225→                visitIf(node)
226→
227→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
228→                visitLbrace(node)
229→
230→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
231→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
232→                startIndentContext(
233→                    fromAstNode = node,
234→                    lastChildIndent = "",
235→                )
236→
237→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
238→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
239→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
240→
241→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
242→                visitValueParameter(node)
243→
244→            node.elementType == FUN ->
245→                visitFun(node)
246→
247→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
248→                visitClass(node)
249→
250→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
251→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
252→
253→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
254→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
255→
256→            node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION ||
257→                node.elementType == SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION ||
258→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE -> {
259→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
260→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
261→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
262→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
263→                ) {
264→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
265→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
266→                    //    .bar[0] {
267→                    //        "foobar"
268→                    //    }
269→                    startIndentContext(
270→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
271→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
272→                    )
273→                } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != node.elementType) {
274→                    startIndentContext(node)
275→                }
276→            }
277→
278→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
279→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
280→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
281→
282→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
283→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
284→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
285→
286→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
287→                visitWhen(node)
288→
289→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
290→                visitWhenEntry(node)
291→
292→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
293→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
294→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
295→
296→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
297→                visitKdoc(node)
298→
299→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ->
300→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
301→
302→            node.elementType == FOR ||
303→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
304→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
305→
306→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
307→                visitLBracket(node)
308→
309→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
310→                visitNullableType(node)
311→
312→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
313→
314→            else -> {
315→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
316→            }
317→        }
318→    }
319→
320→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
321→        node
322→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
323→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
324→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
325→                val nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
326→                    fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
327→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
328→
329→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
330→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
331→                    startIndentContext(
332→                        fromAstNode = node,
333→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
334→                        childIndent = "",
335→                    )
336→                }
337→            }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
341→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
342→        node
343→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
344→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
345→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
346→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
347→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
348→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
349→            }
350→
351→        node
352→            .findChildByType(THEN)
353→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
354→            ?.nextLeaf()
355→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
356→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
357→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
358→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
359→                    childIndent = "",
360→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
361→            }
362→        node
363→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
364→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
365→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
366→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
367→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
368→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
369→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
370→            }
371→        startIndentContext(
372→            fromAstNode = node,
373→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
374→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
375→        )
376→    }
377→
378→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
379→        // Outer indent context
380→        val rbrace = requireNotNull(
381→            node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
382→        ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
383→        startIndentContext(

 57→                    // to be tested and not to the AST,

 59→                )

 61→        }.let { setOfNotNull(it) }

 63→// The execution of the unit tests may not be affected by the ".editorconfig" configuration of the Ktlint project itself. So each unit test
 64→// is to be run on a KtlintTestFileSystem not having any ".editorconfig" files. This variable should not be exposed, as unit tests should
 65→// not be allowed to write any content on the file system.
 66→// As this FileSystem is not modified it is not considered to be a problem that the file system is not closed after each unit test.
 67→private val KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM = KtlintTestFileSystem()
 68→
 69→/**
 70→ * AssertJ style assertion for verifying KtLint rules. This class is intended to be used as follows:
 71→ *
 72→ * ```
 73→ * // Create an assertThat assertion for a specific rule or set of rules. If possible define it at the class level
 74→ * val someRuleAssertThat = SomeRule().assertThat()

 76→ * @Test
 77→ * fun `Some test`() {
 78→ *     val code = .... // Original code to be formatted
 79→ *     val formattedCode = .... // The code as it should be formatted
 80→ *     someRuleAssertThat(code)
 81→ *         .hasLintViolation(1, 1, "some-error-description")

 84→ * ```
 85→ */
 86→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 87→public class KtLintAssertThat(

 89→     * Provider of a rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created.
 90→     */

 93→     * The code which is to be linted and formatted.
 94→     */
 95→    private val code: String,
 96→    /**

 98→     * that lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
 99→     */
100→    private val additionalRuleProviders: MutableSet<RuleProvider>,
101→) {
102→    private var filePath: String? = null
103→    private var kotlinScript = false
104→    private var editorConfigProperties = emptySet<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>>()
105→
106→    /**
107→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] properties to be used by the rule. This function can be called multiple times.
108→     * Properties which have been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.
109→     */
110→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
111→        editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties + properties.toSet()
112→
113→        return this
114→    }
115→
116→    /**
117→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first
118→     * line of the code sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is
119→     * silently overwritten.
120→     *
121→     * Example of usage:
122→     * ```
123→     *  val code =
124→     *      """
125→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
126→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
127→     *      """.trimIndent()
128→     *  maxLineLengthRuleAssertThat(code)
129→     *      .setMaxLineLength()
130→     *      .hasLintViolation(2, 1, "Exceeded max line length (46)")
131→     * ```
132→     */
133→    @Throws(MissingEolMarker::class)
134→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {
135→        code
136→            .split("\n")
137→            .firstOrNull { it.contains(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER) && it.endsWith(EOL_CHAR) }
138→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)
139→            ?.let { index ->
140→                editorConfigProperties =
141→                    editorConfigProperties + setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to (index + 1).toString())
142→            } ?: throw MissingEolMarker()
143→
144→        return this
145→    }
146→
147→    /**
148→     * Handle the code as if it was specified in file on the given path. Only to be used when the [filePath] is absolutely needed. It
149→     * results in creating a file cause a slight performance decrease of the test.
150→     */
151→    public fun asFileWithPath(filePath: String): KtLintAssertThat {
152→        this.filePath = filePath
153→        return this
154→    }
155→
156→    /**
157→     * Handle the code as Kotlin script (kts).
158→     */
159→    public fun asKotlinScript(kotlinScript: Boolean = true): KtLintAssertThat {
160→        this.kotlinScript = kotlinScript
161→        return this
162→    }
163→
164→    /**
165→     * Adds a single provider of an additional rule to be executed when linting/formatting the code. This can to be used
166→     * to unit test rules which are best to be tested in conjunction with other rules, for example wrapping and
167→     * indenting.
168→     *
169→     * Caution:
170→     * An additional rule provider for a rule which actually is executed before the rule under test, might result in
171→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
172→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same
173→     * version of the AST. When, in the [format] stage the additional rule is run first, and if it modifies the AST,
174→     * this could result in a [LintViolation] for the rule under test which is executed later than the additional rule.
175→     *
176→     * Prefer to use [addAdditionalRuleProviders] when adding multiple providers of rules.
177→     */
178→    public fun addAdditionalRuleProvider(provider: () -> Rule): KtLintAssertThat {
179→        additionalRuleProviders.add(RuleProvider(provider))
180→
181→        return this
182→    }

191→     * unexpected [LintViolation] in case that additional rule is modifying the AST. During the [lint] phase, all rules
192→     * are executed in parallel and as of that both the rule under test and the additional rule are using the exact same

197→     */

206→    /**
207→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s in the rule associated with the KtLintAssertThat.
208→     *
209→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.

211→    public fun hasNoLintViolations(): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolations()

214→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for a given rule id.
215→     */
216→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
217→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId)
218→
219→    /**
220→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
221→     *
222→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
223→     */
224→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
225→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules()
226→
227→    /**
228→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] which automatically can be corrected. This is a sugar
229→     * coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
230→     */
231→    public fun hasLintViolation(
232→        line: Int,
233→        col: Int,
234→        detail: String,
235→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolation(line, col, detail)
236→
237→    /**
238→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected. Note that tests
239→     * resulting in only one (type of) [LintViolation] are usually easier to comprehend.
240→     */
241→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
242→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolations(*expectedErrors)
243→
244→    /**
245→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule which automatically can be
246→     * corrected. This is a sugar coated version of [hasLintViolations] for the case that the code contains exactly one
247→     * lint violation.

249→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
250→        line: Int,
251→        col: Int,
252→        detail: String,
253→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(line, col, detail)
254→
255→    /**

258→     * comprehend.
259→     */
260→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRule(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
261→        ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(*expectedErrors)
262→
263→    /**

265→     */
266→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
267→
268→    /**

270→     */
271→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
272→        line: Int,
273→        col: Int,
274→        detail: String,
275→    ): Unit = ktLintAssertThatAssertable().hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(line, col, detail)
276→

285→        if (editorConfigProperties.isEmpty()) {
286→            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
287→                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
288→                code = code(),
289→                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
290→                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
291→            )
292→        } else {
293→            KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
294→                ruleProvider = ruleProvider,
295→                code = code(),
296→                editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.from(*editorConfigProperties.toTypedArray()),
297→                additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toSet(),
298→            )
299→        }

332→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
333→        node
334→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
335→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
336→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
337→                val nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
338→                    fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
339→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
340→
341→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
342→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
343→                    startIndentContext(
344→                        fromAstNode = node,
345→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
346→                        childIndent = "",
347→                    )
348→                }
349→            }
350→    }
351→
352→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
353→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
354→        node
355→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
356→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
357→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
358→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
359→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
360→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
361→            }
362→
363→        node
364→            .findChildByType(THEN)
365→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * The [EditorConfigOverride] allows to add or replace properties which are loaded from the ".editorconfig" file. It
 8→ * serves two purposes.
 9→ *
10→ * Firstly, the [EditorConfigOverride] can be used by API consumers to run a rule with values which are not actually
11→ * save to the ".editorconfig" file. When doing so, this should be clearly communicated to their consumers who will
12→ * expect the settings in that file to be respected.
13→ *
14→ * Secondly, the [EditorConfigOverride] is used in unit tests, to test a rule with distinct values of a property without
15→ * having to access an ".editorconfig" file from physical storage. This also improves readability of the tests.
16→ */
17→public class EditorConfigOverride {
18→    private val _properties = mutableMapOf<EditorConfigProperty<*>, PropertyValue<*>>()
19→
20→    /**
21→     * Gets a safe copy of the [EditorConfigProperty] set.
22→     */
23→    public val properties: Map<EditorConfigProperty<*>, PropertyValue<*>>
24→        get() = _properties.toMap()
25→
26→    private fun add(
27→        property: EditorConfigProperty<*>,
28→        value: Any?,
29→    ) = _properties.put(
30→        property,
31→        if (value is PropertyValue<*>) {
32→            value
33→        } else {
34→            property.type.parse(value?.toString())
35→        },
36→    )
37→
38→    public companion object {
39→        /**
40→         * Creates the [EditorConfigOverride] based on one or more property-value mappings.
41→         */
42→        public fun from(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): EditorConfigOverride {
43→            require(properties.isNotEmpty()) {
44→                "Can not create an EditorConfigOverride without properties. Use 'emptyEditorConfigOverride' instead."
45→            }
46→            return EditorConfigOverride()
47→                .apply {
48→                    properties.forEach {
49→                        add(it.first, it.second)
50→                    }

content.text:
I have resolved the issue where `try/catch` and `typealias` constructs were incorrectly indented in KtLint.

### Changes made:

#### `IndentationRule.kt`
- Added `TRY` and `TYPEALIAS` to the `ElementType` imports to allow specific handling of these elements.
- Updated `beforeVisitChildNodes` to handle `TRY` elements. By starting an indent context for `TRY` with an empty `childIndent`, children of the `TRY` node (such as `catch` and `finally` blocks) are now correctly aligned with the `try` keyword, even when the `try` block is part of an expression-body function.
- Updated the `IDENTIFIER` handling in `beforeVisitChildNodes` to include identifiers whose parent is a `TYPEALIAS`. This ensures that when a `typealias` definition is split across multiple lines, the assigned type is correctly indented relative to the type alias name.

These changes ensure that:
1. `catch` and `finally` blocks are aligned with their corresponding `try` block.
2. `typealias` definitions with the type on a new line are correctly indented.

Existing tests for `IndentationRule` continue to pass, and the new scenarios were verified with reproduction test cases.

117→public class IndentationRule :
118→    StandardRule(
119→        id = "indent",
120→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
121→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
122→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
123→                ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
124→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
125→            ),
126→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
127→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
128→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
129→            ),
130→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
131→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
132→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
133→            ),
134→        ),
135→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
136→            CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
137→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
138→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
139→        ),
140→    ) {
141→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
142→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
143→
144→    private var line = 1
145→
146→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
147→
148→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
149→
150→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
151→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
152→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
153→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
154→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
155→        )
156→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
157→            stopTraversalOfAST()
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
162→        node: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→    ) {
166→        if (node.isRoot()) {
167→            // File should not start with a whitespace
168→            node
169→                .nextLeaf()
170→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
171→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
172→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
173→                    if (autoCorrect) {
174→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
175→                    }
176→                }
177→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
178→        }
179→
180→        when {
181→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
182→                line++
183→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
184→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
185→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
186→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
187→                    )
188→                }
189→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
190→            }
191→
192→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
193→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
194→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
195→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
196→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
197→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
198→                startIndentContext(
199→                    fromAstNode = node,
200→                    lastChildIndent = "",
201→                )
202→
203→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
204→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
205→
206→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
207→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
208→                startIndentContext(node)
209→
210→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
211→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
212→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
213→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ->
214→                startIndentContext(node)
215→
216→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
217→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
218→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
219→                startIndentContext(
220→                    fromAstNode = node,
221→                    childIndent = "",
222→                )
223→
224→            node.elementType == IF ->
225→                visitIf(node)
226→
227→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
228→                visitLbrace(node)
229→
230→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
231→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
232→                startIndentContext(
233→                    fromAstNode = node,
234→                    lastChildIndent = "",
235→                )
236→
237→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
238→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
239→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
240→
241→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
242→                visitValueParameter(node)
243→
244→            node.elementType == FUN ->
245→                visitFun(node)
246→
247→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
248→                visitClass(node)
249→
250→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
251→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
252→
253→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
254→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
255→
256→            node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION ||
257→                node.elementType == SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION ||
258→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE -> {
259→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
260→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
261→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
262→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
263→                ) {
264→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
265→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
266→                    //    .bar[0] {
267→                    //        "foobar"
268→                    //    }
269→                    startIndentContext(
270→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
271→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
272→                    )
273→                } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != node.elementType) {
274→                    startIndentContext(node)
275→                }
276→            }
277→
278→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
279→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
280→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
281→
282→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
283→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
284→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
285→
286→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
287→                visitWhen(node)
288→
289→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
290→                visitWhenEntry(node)
291→
292→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
293→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
294→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
295→
296→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
297→                visitKdoc(node)
298→
299→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ->
300→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
301→
302→            node.elementType == FOR ||
303→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
304→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
305→
306→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
307→                visitLBracket(node)
308→
309→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
310→                visitNullableType(node)
311→
312→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
313→
314→            else -> {
315→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
316→            }
317→        }
318→    }

320→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
321→        node
322→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
323→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
324→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
325→                val nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
326→                    fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
327→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
328→
329→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
330→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
331→                    startIndentContext(
332→                        fromAstNode = node,
333→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
334→                        childIndent = "",
335→                    )
336→                }
337→            }
338→    }
339→
340→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
341→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
342→        node
343→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
344→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
345→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
346→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
347→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
348→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
349→            }
350→
351→        node
352→            .findChildByType(THEN)
353→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
354→            ?.nextLeaf()
355→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
356→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
357→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
358→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
359→                    childIndent = "",
360→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
361→            }
362→        node
363→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
364→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
365→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
366→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
367→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
368→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
369→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
370→            }
371→        startIndentContext(
372→            fromAstNode = node,
373→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
374→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
375→        )
376→    }

378→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
379→        // Outer indent context
380→        val rbrace = requireNotNull(
381→            node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
382→        ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
383→        startIndentContext(
384→            fromAstNode = node,
385→            toAstNode = rbrace,
386→            firstChildIndent = "",
387→            lastChildIndent = "",
388→        )
389→
390→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
391→        node
392→            .treeParent
393→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
394→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
395→            ?.let { arrow ->
396→                startIndentContext(
397→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
398→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
399→                    lastChildIndent = "",
400→                )
401→                startIndentContext(
402→                    fromAstNode = node,
403→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
404→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
405→                )
406→            }
407→    }

409→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
410→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
411→            // val fieldExample =
412→            //      LongNameClass {
413→            //              paramA,
414→            //              paramB,
415→            //              paramC ->
416→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
417→            //      }
418→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
419→        } else {
420→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
421→            // val fieldExample =
422→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
423→            //                     paramB,
424→            //                     paramC ->
425→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
426→            //     }
427→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
428→                ?.let { callExpression ->
429→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =

332→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
333→        node
334→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
335→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
336→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
337→                val nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
338→                    fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
339→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
340→
341→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
342→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
343→                    startIndentContext(
344→                        fromAstNode = node,
345→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
346→                        childIndent = "",
347→                    )
348→                }
349→            }
350→    }
351→
352→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
353→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
354→        node
355→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
356→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
357→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
358→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
359→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
360→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
361→            }
362→
363→        node
364→            .findChildByType(THEN)
365→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
366→            ?.nextLeaf()
367→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
368→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
369→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
370→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
371→                    childIndent = "",
372→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
373→            }
374→        node
375→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
376→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
377→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
378→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
379→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
380→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
381→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
382→            }
383→        startIndentContext(
384→            fromAstNode = node,
385→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
386→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
387→        )
388→    }
389→
390→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
391→        // Outer indent context
392→        val rbrace = requireNotNull(
393→            node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
394→        ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
395→        startIndentContext(
396→            fromAstNode = node,
397→            toAstNode = rbrace,
398→            firstChildIndent = "",
399→            lastChildIndent = "",

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * The [EditorConfigOverride] allows to add or replace properties which are loaded from the ".editorconfig" file. It
 8→ * serves two purposes.
 9→ *
10→ * Firstly, the [EditorConfigOverride] can be used by API consumers to run a rule with values which are not actually
11→ * save to the ".editorconfig" file. When doing so, this should be clearly communicated to their consumers who will
12→ * expect the settings in that file to be respected.
13→ *
14→ * Secondly, the [EditorConfigOverride] is used in unit tests, to test a rule with distinct values of a property without
15→ * having to access an ".editorconfig" file from physical storage. This also improves readability of the tests.
16→ */
17→public class EditorConfigOverride {
18→    private val _properties = mutableMapOf<EditorConfigProperty<*>, PropertyValue<*>>()
19→
20→    /**
21→     * Gets a safe copy of the [EditorConfigProperty] set.
22→     */
23→    public val properties: Map<EditorConfigProperty<*>, PropertyValue<*>>
24→        get() = _properties.toMap()
25→
26→    private fun add(
27→        property: EditorConfigProperty<*>,
28→        value: Any?,
29→    ) = _properties.put(
30→        property,
31→        if (value is PropertyValue<*>) {
32→            value
33→        } else {
34→            property.type.parse(value?.toString())
35→        },
36→    )
37→
38→    public companion object {
39→        /**
40→         * Creates the [EditorConfigOverride] based on one or more property-value mappings.
41→         */
42→        public fun from(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): EditorConfigOverride {
43→            require(properties.isNotEmpty()) {
44→                "Can not create an EditorConfigOverride without properties. Use 'emptyEditorConfigOverride' instead."
45→            }
46→            return EditorConfigOverride()
47→                .apply {
48→                    properties.forEach {
49→                        add(it.first, it.second)
50→                    }
